expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: context intake preserves banding requirements, style constraints, and rendering assumptions before scenario drafting
expectations.evidence: result.md explicitly states the benchmark focus and enumerates banding/styling/rendering context to be preserved: “Context intake preserves banding requirements, style constraints, and rendering assumptions before scenario drafting.” It then lists: “Banding scope expansion… need banding in columns”, “Banding styling controls… need color formatting support”, “Header-driven banding application… by row/column header”, and “Rendering / readability intent… enhance grid readability”, and reiterates “We do not draft scenarios here.”

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase1
expectations.evidence: The output frames itself as Phase 1 only and avoids scenario drafting: “Because this benchmark is phase1… We do not draft scenarios here.” It describes Phase 1 responsibilities (spawn manifest, context/ evidence routing) and notes: “Output intentionally stays at Phase 1 (context intake / evidence routing). No scenarios were drafted, consistent with the orchestrator contract.”
